description:
You are a monitoring-platform specialist who has just received a draft JSON
    alert feed and its accompanying JSON-Schema.  
    Both files already exist on disk:

      1. /home/user/monitoring/alert-schema.json  
      2. /home/user/monitoring/alerts.json  

    Your objectives are:

      A. Validate every element of /home/user/monitoring/alerts.json against
         /home/user/monitoring/alert-schema.json.
      B. Count how many alert objects pass the schema check and how many fail.
      C. Create two output artefacts, **and nothing else**:

         i.  /home/user/monitoring/alert_validation.log  
             • Exactly two lines, **no extra whitespace, no blank lines**.  
             • First line:  VALID=<integer>  
             • Second line: INVALID=<integer>  

         ii. /home/user/monitoring/critical_alerts.json  
             • A JSON array (pretty-printed or compact, either is fine)  
             • It must contain only those alert objects whose  
               "severity" field equals the literal string "critical" **and**
               which were also reported as schema-valid in step A.

    Constraints & tips:

      • Stay inside /home/user/monitoring; you do NOT need root privileges.  
      • You may use any CLI validator available in the container  
        (e.g. `jsonschema`, `ajv`, `python -m jsonschema`, etc.).  
      • Feel free to employ `jq` to filter or transform JSON; the whole task
        can be completed in just two or three commands.  
      • The automated checker will fail if:
          – alert_validation.log deviates from the required two-line format  
          – the VALID / INVALID counts are wrong  
          – critical_alerts.json is missing or includes the wrong objects  
          – any extra files are created in /home/user/monitoring

    Deliverables after you finish:

      1. /home/user/monitoring/alert_validation.log  
      2. /home/user/monitoring/critical_alerts.json
truth:
<!-- These files already exist before the agent starts -->
    <preexisting_files>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alert-schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "service", "severity", "timestamp"],
  "properties": {
    "id":        { "type": "string" },
    "service":   { "type": "string" },
    "severity":  { "type": "string",
                   "enum": ["low", "medium", "high", "critical"] },
    "timestamp": { "type": "string",
                   "format": "date-time" }
  },
  "additionalProperties": false
}
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alerts.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {
    "id": "a1",
    "service": "auth",
    "severity": "high",
    "timestamp": "2023-09-12T10:33:00Z"
  },
  {
    "id": "a2",
    "service": "billing",
    "severity": "critical",
    "timestamp": "2023-09-12T10:35:27Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 100,
    "service": "db",
    "severity": "urgent",
    "timestamp": "not a date"
  }
]
        ]]></file>
    </preexisting_files>

    <!-- Expected artefacts created by agent -->
    <expected_outputs>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alert_validation.log"><![CDATA[
VALID=2
INVALID=1
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/critical_alerts.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {
    "id": "a2",
    "service": "billing",
    "severity": "critical",
    "timestamp": "2023-09-12T10:35:27Z"
  }
]
        ]]></file>
    </expected_outputs>
